title: Detecting Stationary Measure Classes
speaker: Kunal Chawla
abstract: A random walk on a hyperbolic group G will converge almost surely to the boundary, defining a stationary measure at infinity. When G is a cocompact fuchsian group, this boundary is the circle, and when the random walk is finitely supported it is unknown whether this measure can ever be absolutely continuous with respect to the Lebesgue measure. We provide a simple and geometric family of statistics which determine whether two stationary measures are in the same measure class.

proof_stages.label: Random-walk harmonic measures on hyperbolic boundaries
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up a finitely generated group with a generating probability measure, recalls almost-sure convergence of random-walk orbits to the boundary of a hyperbolic space, and defines the resulting stationary hitting measure.

proof_stages.label: Singularity problem and known landscape
proof_stages.summary: The speaker motivates the problem of comparing harmonic measure with natural boundary measure classes, surveys known singularity results and counterexamples, and narrows attention to finitely supported measures on cocompact lattices in rank one.

proof_stages.label: Green-metric criterion and cocompact gap
proof_stages.summary: Existing work is organized around the Green distance: mutual absolute continuity of boundary measures is related to rough similarity between the Green metric and a geometric or word metric, but cocompact applications remain scarce and ad hoc.

proof_stages.label: Subadditive translated-segment counting statistics
proof_stages.summary: For each group element and discount parameter, the speaker defines a path cost C_{g,d} counting translated copies of a fixed geodesic segment, proves subadditive normalized limits along random walks, and observes that unequal normalized limits force singularity of harmonic measures.

proof_stages.label: Detection theorem via equality of all statistics
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that, in the proper hyperbolic bottleneck setting, absolute continuity of two harmonic measure classes is equivalent to equality of all normalized C_{g,d} statistics, with an analogous equivalence for limiting variances.

proof_stages.label: Metric, winding, and quasimorphism consequences
proof_stages.summary: The theorem and its method recover bounded-density and rescaled Green-distance equivalences, connect to winding statistics, and extend naturally from the C_{g,d} functions to quasimorphisms and possibly to other Patterson/Gibbs-type boundary measure classes.

proof_stages.label: Probabilistic proof mechanism from singularity
proof_stages.summary: The proof sketch starts from a boundary set witnessing singularity, turns its hitting probabilities under the two walks into martingales, stops them after stabilization, and uses a covering/pigeonhole argument plus hyperbolic geometry to produce a detecting statistic.

proof_stages.label: Conjectural cocompact-lattice application
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains how the method might attack the finitely supported lattice conjecture in SL(2,R), using crossing hyperbolic elements and metric non-additivity, and relates the conjecture to classical negatively curved Riemannian geometry.
